topic: Void Editor Review 2026: Open-Source Cursor Alternative with Local Models

key_points: InfoQ covered Void's beta launch in June 2025, positioning it as a privacy-focused free alternative to Cursor
Highlighted that Void processes AI locally or via direct API calls, eliminating third-party intermediary
Noted community enthusiasm but also skepticism about 'proliferation of VS Code forks'
Microsoft announced plans to open-source GitHub Copilot Chat Extension, potentially narrowing the gap
Comprehensive comparison: Void is MIT/Apache 2.0 licensed vs Cursor's proprietary model
Void sends messages directly editor-to-provider; Cursor routes code through its own backend
Void lacks documentation indexing (URL crawling + embeddings), which Cursor has
Verdict: 'Acceptable for indie projects and research; likely disqualifying for regulated enterprise deployments'
Development is paused as of April 22, 2026 verification — key risk factor
'If you've ever had to answer where does our source code go in a security review, this is the shortest answer in the category'
Cursor has ~800K monthly active users (2026), market leader, from €18/month
Void: Free (MIT license), open source, supports any LLM including Ollama/local models
Void verdict: 'Best choice for developers who want Cursor functionality with full data control and open-source transparency'
Cursor verdict: 'Most flexible and powerful AI code editor — ideal for professional developers who need maximum control and model choice'
Cursor Pro+ from €55/month; Void is completely free (pay only your provider API costs)
'Cursor is a black box; you send code to their servers (mostly), and you pay them a subscription. Void runs entirely locally or connects to your own API keys'
Void described as best for 'privacy absolutists' and 'open source zealots'
Cursor criticized for performance on monorepos, cost complexity, and workflow lock-in in 2026
'In 2026, the answer to which AI editor should I use is: It depends. The era of one size fits all IDE is ending'
Many professionals combine Claude Code for heavy lifting with Cursor or Copilot for daily coding
Video title: 'The Open Source Cursor Alternative Has Failed (Void)' by Chris Hawkes (August 25, 2025)
1,611 views, 52 likes — critical review noting project disbandment
Local models critique: 'You have to have a pretty damn good machine to run Qwen 2.5 14B. If you don't have a 4080, it's going to be dog slow'
Discord community showing 'they love the idea, but it doesn't work very well'
Critique based on pre-pause state but prophetically titled

stats.fact: Void editor has 28,800 GitHub stars and 2,500 forks as of May 2026
Void development was officially paused on January 12, 2026, with the last release being v1.4.9 on June 23, 2025
Void's official paused statement: 'We've paused work on the Void IDE to explore a few novel coding ideas. We want to focus on innovation over feature-parity'
Cursor has approximately 800,000 monthly active users in 2026, making it the market leader in AI code editors
Cursor pricing ranges from €18/month (Pro) to €55/month (Pro+); Void is completely free with user-provided API keys
Void was backed by Y Combinator (YC) and founded by Andrew Pareles and Mathew Pareles
Void has 46 contributors and 2,771 total commits since its initial commit on September 10, 2024
Hacker News submission of Void received 948 points and 343 comments (May 8, 2025)
Cursor acquired Supermaven in November 2024, gaining ~250ms autocomplete latency technology (3x faster than competitors at the time)
Void codebase is 95.3% TypeScript, making it highly accessible for JavaScript/TypeScript developers who want to contribute

angles: The privacy-first developer's dream: Void routes zero code through third-party servers — every API call goes directly from editor to provider
The $0 AI IDE: how Void delivers Cursor-like features for the cost of your own API keys (often cheaper than any subscription for light users)
Development pause reality check: what the January 2026 pause means for teams considering Void, and whether it's still safe to use
Local model deep dive: running DeepSeek, Llama 3.1, and Qwen locally via Ollama for 100% offline AI coding
VS Code fork wars: why Void, Cursor, and Windsurf all chose to fork VSCode rather than build extensions — and what it means for users
Open source AI IDE maturity ladder: Void vs Cline vs Continue vs Zed — which open-source option is actually ready for production?
YC-backed and then paused: the story of Void's rise, pivot, and what comes next for open-source AI editors
Void's unique Gather Mode: read-only agent mode that can scan your codebase without making changes — a feature Cursor doesn't have
keywords: bring your own API key IDE
outline: H2: What Is Void Editor? (The Open-Source Cursor Fork Explained)
H2: Void Editor Key Features in 2026
H2: Local Model Support: Running Ollama, DeepSeek, and Llama Locally
H2: Void Editor vs Cursor: Head-to-Head Feature Comparison
H2: Void Editor vs Other Open-Source Alternatives (Cline, Continue, Zed)
H2: Privacy and Data Control: Why Void's Architecture Is Different
H2: The January 2026 Development Pause: What It Means for Users
H2: Performance and Hardware Requirements for Local Models
H2: How to Get Started with Void Editor
H2: Who Should Use Void Editor in 2026?
H2: Final Verdict: Is Void Editor Still Worth It?

additional_context.development_status: PAUSED as of January 12, 2026. Last release v1.4.9 on June 23, 2025. Official site shows 'Work on Void is currently paused. Some features may be outdated or broken.'
additional_context.team: Andrew Pareles and Mathew Pareles (co-founders), 46 contributors total
unique_features: Gather Mode: read-only agent that can scan codebase without making changes
Checkpoints: every AI-driven file modification is checkpointed for rollback without Git stashes
Fast Apply: structured patch application rather than full file regeneration
FIM (Fill-in-the-Middle) support for autocomplete (technically correct API shape)
Prompt inspection: users can view and edit underlying prompts sent to AI
No backend middleman: all API calls go directly from editor to provider
missing_vs_cursor: Documentation indexing (URL crawling + embeddings for API docs)
Background Agents (cloud sandboxes for parallel async tasks)
